title: MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN)
text:
MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN) - SupportGoWhere
MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN)
Share link
All Singapore Citizen newborns are eligible for the MediSave Grant for Newborns (MGN) of up to $5,000.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
MediSave Grant of $5,000 for Singapore Citizen Newborns
4% annual interest
Grant can cover MediShield Life premiums, vaccinations, hospitalisation and outpatient treatments
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to $5,000 credited to each Singapore Citizen newborn's CPF MediSave account

Accounts are opened automatically for newborns

Covers MediShield Life premiums, recommended childhood vaccinations, hospitalisation, and approved outpatient treatments

Who is this for?

All Singapore Citizen newborns born on or after 26 August 2012 are eligible for MGN

Children who are Singapore Citizens at birth and born before January 1, 2015, are entitled to MGN of up to $3,000

For those born on or after January 1, 2015, they are eligible for MGN of $4,000

For those born on or after April 1, 2025, they are eligible for MGN of $5,000

Children who are born overseas can qualify for MGN upon registering their birth with Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA) or through a Singapore Overseas Mission

The MGN amount will depend on the date that the child becomes a Singapore Citizen

Children who obtain Singapore Citizenship after the age of 2 are not eligible for MGN.

How to apply?

No application required

The grant is automatically credited into your newborn’s MediSave account within 1 week from birth registration / acquiring Singapore citizenship

Once the MGN has been credited, you will receive a notification on the MGN amount

You may also check your child’s CPF statement online by logging into your CPF with your Singpass.

Where can I find help?

View more details

Healthcare Hotline: 1800-222-3399
For overseas callers: +65 6222 3399

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch

Note: By appointment only

Scheme last updated 05 Mar 2026
